title: Events, Refs, SHAs, Forks, and Workflow Trust Boundaries · Guided Workflow Studio
summary: A delivery pairing session predicts each automation transition before producing the first working event and revision trust policy for events, refs, shas, forks, and workflow trust boundaries. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of event and revision trust policy while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Derive workflow source, checked-out revision, GITHUB_REF, GITHUB_SHA, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger.
Choose pull request merge, head, or base revision intentionally and state what integration or source claim each can prove.
Model fork approvals, withheld secrets, read-only tokens, compute abuse, contributor changes, and re-run authority.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Event Payload Ref Sha
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop maintainer runs fork code through pull_request_target with write authority, then a privileged workflow_run consumes its artifact. Establish workflow source, tested revision, actor, permissions, producer trust, and data-only inspection.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Event Payload Ref Sha.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for derive workflow source, checked-out revision, github_ref, github_sha, actor, permissions, and payload from each trigger. For Commit to the mission model · Workshop Ci Event Payload Ref Sha, keep Ci Event Payload Ref Sha connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust
steps.instruction:
Append the CI/CD evidence item headed "  # Evidence: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust-bb41xo". Tailor its event-trust-policy.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic workflow or release review, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run GitHub Actions, runner, Docker, registry, OIDC, Google Cloud, and deployment work later in authorized disposable transfer environments.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust, keep Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust-bb41xo
  - competency: ci-workflow-run-artifact-trust
    artifact: event-trust-policy.yaml
    decision: workflow run artifact trust
    environment: GitHub-hosted ubuntu-24.04 runner on a cold cache
    fault-injection: restore a cache written from a lower-trust branch
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: bb41xo
    change: |
      event: pull_request
      workflow-source: merge-commit
      checkout-revision: pull-request-merge-sha
      token: read-only
      secrets: withheld
      untrusted-code: isolated-execution
      privileged-followup: data-only
      producer-proof: run-id-and-workflow-sha
    verify: compare pull_request pull_request_target workflow_run refs SHAs tokens secrets and checked-out code
    failure: Reject fork code under pull_request_target secrets, maintainer rerun as trust, or successful artifacts as executable proof.
    repair: Use low-privilege pull_request execution, separate privileged follow-up, and validate producer and content strictly as data.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The Ci Workflow Run Artifact Trust structural check is only one gate. event and revision trust policy must also survive the stated changed case and causal explanation.
